edges.description: Impaired mandibular identity specification disrupts condylar development.
Mandibular hypoplasia results from abnormal first arch-derived jaw patterning.
External ear malformation accompanies first and second arch patterning defects.
Developmental patterning defects converge on the EDN1-DLX5/6 mandibular identity program.
GNAI3 acts as an intracellular effector in the EDN1-EDNRA developmental signaling pathway.
PLCB4 acts as an intracellular effector in the EDN1-EDNRA developmental signaling pathway.
TWIST1 regulatory dysregulation impairs neural crest migration and craniofacial differentiation programs.

nodes.description: Cranial neural crest cells populate the pharyngeal arches and generate most craniofacial bone, cartilage, and connective tissue. In auriculocondylar syndrome, disturbed neural crest identity and patterning underlie ear and mandibular malformations.

evidence.reference_title: Understanding the basis of auriculocondylar syndrome: Insights from human, mouse and zebrafish genetic studies.
evidence.snippet: Affected structures arise from cranial neural crest cells, a population of cells in the embryo that reside in the pharyngeal arches and give rise to most of the bone, cartilage and connective tissue of the face.

evidence.reference_title: Understanding the basis of auriculocondylar syndrome: Insights from human, mouse and zebrafish genetic studies.
evidence.snippet: Disruption of this signaling pathway in both mouse and zebrafish results in loss of identity of neural crest cells of the mandibular portion of the first pharyngeal arch and the subsequent repatterning of these cells, leading to homeosis of lower jaw structures into more maxillary-like structures.

nodes.description: Mandibular distraction osteogenesis can be part of staged treatment for severe mandibular and airway-related dentofacial deformity.

evidence.reference_title: Novel GNAI3 mutation in a Chinese family with auriculocondylar syndrome and treatment of severe dentofacial deformities: a 5-year follow-up case report.
evidence.snippet: Sequential therapy with preoperative orthodontic treatment combined with distraction osteogenesis and orthognathic surgery guided by 3D digital technology may be a practical and effective method for treating ARCND.

nodes.description: GNAI3 variants cluster around GDP/GTP-binding motifs and are proposed to act through a dominant-negative mechanism that interferes with endothelin pathway signal transduction during pharyngeal arch development.

evidence.reference_title: Novel variants in GNAI3 associated with auriculocondylar syndrome strengthen a common dominant negative effect.
evidence.snippet: Structural modeling shows that all five altered GNAI3 residues identified to date cluster in a region involved in GDP/GTP binding.

evidence.reference_title: Novel variants in GNAI3 associated with auriculocondylar syndrome strengthen a common dominant negative effect.
evidence.snippet: We hypothesize that all GNAI3 variants lead to dominant negative effects.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in endothelin 1 cause recessive auriculocondylar syndrome and dominant isolated question-mark ears.
evidence.snippet: Targeted sequencing of EDN1 in an ACS individual with related parents identified a fourth, homozygous mutation falling close to the site of cleavage by endothelin-converting enzyme.

nodes.description: The central mechanism is impaired endothelin 1 signaling during early pharyngeal arch patterning. EDN1 ligand, EDNRA receptor signaling, and downstream GNAI3 and PLCB4 effectors help specify mandibular identity in cranial neural crest-derived tissues; disruption reduces downstream DLX5/DLX6 expression and repatterns mandibular structures toward a more maxillary-like identity.

evidence.reference_title: A human homeotic transformation resulting from mutations in PLCB4 and GNAI3 causes auriculocondylar syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Functional studies demonstrated a significant reduction in downstream DLX5 and DLX6 expression in ACS cases in assays using cultured osteoblasts from probands and controls.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in endothelin 1 cause recessive auriculocondylar syndrome and dominant isolated question-mark ears.
evidence.snippet: These findings provide further support for the hypothesis that ACS and QMEs are uniquely caused by disruption of the EDN1-EDNRA signaling pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Novel variants in GNAI3 associated with auriculocondylar syndrome strengthen a common dominant negative effect.
evidence.snippet: Causative variants have been identified in PLCB4, GNAI3 and EDN1, which are predicted to function within the EDN1-EDNRA pathway during early pharyngeal arch patterning.

evidence.reference_title: Novel GNAI3 mutation in a Chinese family with auriculocondylar syndrome and treatment of severe dentofacial deformities: a 5-year follow-up case report.
evidence.snippet: A novel insertional mutation in the guanine nucleotide-binding protein alpha-inhibiting activity polypeptide 3 (GNAI3) was identified in the patient and their brother using whole-exome sequencing.

nodes.description: Underdevelopment of the mandibular condyle is part of the defining clinical triad.

evidence.reference_title: Novel GNAI3 mutation in a Chinese family with auriculocondylar syndrome and treatment of severe dentofacial deformities: a 5-year follow-up case report.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Auriculocondylar syndrome (ARCND) is an extremely rare autosomal dominant or recessive condition that typically manifests as question mark ears (QMEs), mandibular condyle hypoplasia, and micrognathia.

evidence.reference_title: Targeted molecular investigation in patients within the clinical spectrum of Auriculocondylar syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Auriculocondylar syndrome, mainly characterized by micrognathia, small mandibular condyle, and question mark ears, is a rare disease segregating in an autosomal dominant pattern in the majority of the families reported in the literature.

nodes.description: Developmental mandibular hypoplasia is a core clinical feature.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of auriculocondylar syndrome with a novel missense variant of GNAI3: a case report.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Auriculocondylar syndrome (ACS) is a rare disorder characterized by micrognathia, mandibular condyle hypoplasia, and auricular abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of auriculocondylar syndrome with a novel missense variant of GNAI3: a case report.
evidence.snippet: Severe micrognathia and mandibular hypoplasia were identified on ultrasonography.

nodes.description: Severe dentofacial deformities may be treated with staged craniofacial surgery, including distraction osteogenesis and orthognathic surgery, often guided by three-dimensional digital planning.

evidence.reference_title: Novel GNAI3 mutation in a Chinese family with auriculocondylar syndrome and treatment of severe dentofacial deformities: a 5-year follow-up case report.
evidence.snippet: After a multidisciplinary consultation and examination, sequential orthodontic treatment and craniofacial surgery, including distraction osteogenesis and orthognathic surgery, were performed using three-dimensional (3D) digital technology to treat the patient's dentofacial deformity.

evidence.reference_title: Novel GNAI3 mutation in a Chinese family with auriculocondylar syndrome and treatment of severe dentofacial deformities: a 5-year follow-up case report.
evidence.snippet: Sequential therapy with preoperative orthodontic treatment combined with distraction osteogenesis and orthognathic surgery guided by 3D digital technology may be a practical and effective method for treating ARCND.

nodes.description: PLCB4 variants include heterozygous missense variants clustered in protein domains and biallelic loss-of-function lesions. Heterozygous missense variants are proposed to interfere dominantly rather than act through simple haploinsufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Heterogeneity of mutational mechanisms and modes of inheritance in auriculocondylar syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The narrow distribution of mutations within protein space suggests that the mutations may result in dominantly interfering proteins, rather than haploinsufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Auriculocondylar syndrome 2 caused by a novel PLCB4 variant in a male Chinese neonate: A case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Trio-based whole-exome sequencing identified a novel missense variant of NM_001377142.1:c.1928C>T (NP_001364071.1:p.Ser643Phe) in the PLCB4 gene, which was predicted to impair the local structural stability with a result that the protein function might be affected.

evidence.reference_title: Targeted molecular investigation in patients within the clinical spectrum of Auriculocondylar syndrome.
evidence.snippet: So far, pathogenic variants in PLCB4, GNAI3, and EDN1 have been associated with this syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Auriculocondylar syndrome 2 caused by a novel PLCB4 variant in a male Chinese neonate: A case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: METHODS: This study reports a case of ARCND2 resulting from a novel pathogenic variant in the PLCB4 gene, and summarizes PLCB4 gene mutation sites and phenotypes of ARCND2.

nodes.description: A characteristic external ear malformation with a defect or cleft near the lobe-helix junction; it may be syndromic or, in some EDN1 families, isolated.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in endothelin 1 cause recessive auriculocondylar syndrome and dominant isolated question-mark ears.
evidence.snippet: Auriculocondylar syndrome (ACS) is a rare craniofacial disorder with mandibular hypoplasia and question-mark ears (QMEs) as major features.

evidence.reference_title: Auriculocondylar syndrome 2 caused by a novel PLCB4 variant in a male Chinese neonate: A case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Micrognathia, microstomia, distinctive question mark ears, as well as mandibular condyle hypoplasia were identified.

nodes.description: Severe neonatal mandibular and oral malformations can present with respiratory distress.

evidence.reference_title: Auriculocondylar syndrome 2 caused by a novel PLCB4 variant in a male Chinese neonate: A case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The proband, a 5-day-old male neonate, was referred to our hospital for respiratory distress.

nodes.description: Infants with severe mandibular hypoplasia, microstomia, or respiratory distress require individualized supportive airway, feeding, dental, and craniofacial specialty care.

evidence.reference_title: Auriculocondylar syndrome 2 caused by a novel PLCB4 variant in a male Chinese neonate: A case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The proband, a 5-day-old male neonate, was referred to our hospital for respiratory distress.

nodes.description: A 430 kb duplication involving HDAC9 and TWIST1 regulatory elements can deregulate TWIST1 expression in neural crest cells and impair neural crest migration and osteogenic differentiation.

evidence.reference_title: New locus underlying auriculocondylar syndrome (ARCND): 430 kb duplication involving TWIST1 regulatory elements.
evidence.snippet: We also identified decreased migration of iPSC-derived neural crest cells together with dysregulation of osteogenic differentiation in iPSC-affected mesenchymal stem cells.

evidence.reference_title: New locus underlying auriculocondylar syndrome (ARCND): 430 kb duplication involving TWIST1 regulatory elements.
evidence.snippet: Our findings support the hypothesis that the 430 kb duplication is causative of the ARCND phenotype in this family and that deregulation of TWIST1 expression during craniofacial development can contribute to the phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: New locus underlying auriculocondylar syndrome (ARCND): 430 kb duplication involving TWIST1 regulatory elements.
evidence.snippet: This study highlights a fourth locus causative of ARCND, represented by a tandem duplication of 430 kb in a candidate region on chromosome 7 defined by linkage analysis.
